purpose: The commit message indicates that it fixes a bug in the Bluetooth hci_sync module where commands are queued when HCI_UNREGISTER is set. The technical changes include checking for this flag and returning an error if it's set.

The commit message indicates that it removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h in n_hdlc.c, which is a driver for HDLC protocol support. This change improves code organization and simplifies the dependency structure.
The commit message is clear and concise, but it does not provide enough information about the technical changes or purpose of the commit.

This commit introduces a fix for an issue where double freeing of the structure can occur when unmapping hugepages. It also includes a change to prevent sharing of hugepages if VM_MAYSHARE is not set.
The commit message indicates that it adds AVDD and MICVDD supplies to the binding for a Realtek codec (rt5682s). This is a technical change related to power management and device drivers.

The commit message indicates that a resource leak was fixed in zorro7xx_remove_one(), which is a SCSI driver for Zorro-II and Zorro-III expansion boards. The error handling path of the probe releases a resource that is not freed in the remove function, so this commit adds an iounmap() call to undo the ioremap() operation in the remove function.
The commit message indicates that a work field is removed from struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the net/mlx5e module. This change is made to remove unused code and improve performance.

The commit message indicates that it changes a struct member from unsigned to signed, which is a technical change. The description also mentions that the key_idx isn't used in some cases but can lead to warnings, suggesting that this change addresses an issue or bug.

The commit message indicates that this is a refactoring change to sort CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in the same way as savedefconfig does. This suggests that there are no functional changes and only code organization/readability improvements.
The commit message introduces a new function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to check if the chip's FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode. It also replaces an open coded check with this new function.

The commit message indicates that the purpose of this commit is to skip running tests when RTC (Real-Time Clock) is not present. This suggests a bug-fixing or preventive maintenance aspect, which could be considered as a Bug-Fixing Commit (BFC).

The commit message indicates that a redundant assignment to variable 'bytes' is being removed in the ALSA echoaudio driver code. This change seems to be primarily aimed at improving code quality and reducing unnecessary operations.

This commit decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks, allowing the former to be built without the latter. This is a technical change that improves the flexibility and efficiency of kernel tracing operations.

The commit message indicates that it removes duplicated comments from spte.h, which is a file related to memory management in the Linux kernel. This suggests that the commit has a technical purpose and is not primarily focused on adding new features or fixing bugs.

The commit message indicates that this change is a revert of an earlier change to dcn315 clk table population based on dcfclk. The description explains the reason for reverting the change - it caused underflow issues due to how pmfw fills out the table when dcfclk states are disabled.

The commit message provides a clear description of what the commit does (adding a compatible string for Renesas RZ/V2L SoC) and its technical changes (documenting Mali-G31 GPU). The code changes are also visible in the diff.
The commit message clearly describes the purpose of renaming a struct to make it more consistent with other names in the codebase.
The commit message is clear and concise. It fixes a clang warning in the AMD XGBE driver.

The commit message indicates that it fixes a bug in the MIPS jump_label code by casting an upper bound to long to do signed comparison. The technical changes are clear and include modifications to the jump_label code.

The commit message indicates that it removes a case from SetHwReg8188EU() function, which is part of an ongoing effort to get rid of the unwanted hal layer. This suggests a primary purpose of refactoring or cleanup.

The commit message indicates that it declares non global symbols as static in arm64 architecture to fix sparse warnings introduced while adding errata. This change is likely a bugfix and preventive measure to avoid future issues.
The commit message indicates that this change is related to MDSS interrupt provider nodes. The technical changes involve removing the bogus interrupt flags cell on these nodes.

The commit message indicates that it is preparing for a future change in array size by allocating space for the arrays in struct ctrl_ref. This suggests that the purpose of this commit is to make room for potential changes or additions to the codebase.

The commit message indicates that it adds support for various SoCs (System-on-Chip) and PMICs (Power Management ICs) to the socinfo driver. The technical changes include adding new entries to a list of supported devices.

The commit message indicates that it recognizes all possible chip cuts for a specific WiFi device (RTL8XXXU). This is a technical change that requires understanding of the hardware and software involved. The commit also includes a link to an email thread with more information about the changes.

The commit message indicates that this change is related to a specific clock in the PRCMU (Power and Reset Controller Module Unit) of the UX500 platform. The core already keeps a software enable count, so this change drops the custom software enable count for the PRCMU clocks.
The commit message clearly describes the purpose of the change, including removing #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage in various contexts.

The commit message indicates that it enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware for the Qualcomm Snapdragon SM6350-Lena platform. This is a technical change related to enabling specific hardware features on a specific SoC.

The commit message indicates that it fixes an infinite loop in release_swfw_sync, which is a resource-release function. The technical changes include adding error handling and logging to prevent the infinite loop from occurring.

The commit message indicates that it updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology for clocking. The technical changes include updating the bus clock defines and renaming them to reflect the new terminology.
The commit message indicates that this change removes a redundant check for IS_SWAPFILE, which is always defined. The technical changes are clear and straightforward.

The commit message describes a new feature that implements GTP echo responses to comply with the GTP specification. The technical changes include adding code for handling GTP_ECHO_REQ messages and sending back GTP_ECHO_RSP messages.
The commit message indicates that it replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate. This suggests a technical change to improve the codebase.

The commit message indicates that the code changes are related to removing a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR when roaming from one AP to another. This suggests that the purpose of this change is to fix an issue where the host driver displays an error message on console due to the failed attempt to get TIM information from firmware.
The commit message is clear and concise. It adds support for a specific board configuration without amplifier.

The commit message indicates that it adds a check for zerocopy sendmsg requests on protocols that don't support it. This is a technical change that affects the behavior of the code and could be considered a bug fix or improvement.
The commit message clearly describes the purpose of the change, which is to improve the situation on RHEL 9 and its clones by testing for xeCJK.sty before loading it.
The commit message indicates that it allows 'case' macros to be used in drivers without reporting errors. This suggests a technical change related to code formatting or style.

The commit message indicates that this change is a refactoring of atomic helpers in the simple-KMS code to use regular atomic helpers. The description also mentions that there are no functional changes.

The commit message indicates that a jpeg decoding worker is added to ensure three HWs run in parallel on MT8195. This suggests the commit has technical changes related to hardware and software interaction.

The commit message indicates that it removes pointless VPRINTK() calls from the code, which suggests a bug-fixing purpose. Additionally, the summary mentions removing ata_scsi_dump_cdb(), which is likely related to debugging or logging functionality.

The commit message describes a change to the display driver code that adds support for zstate during extended vblank. This is a technical change that requires understanding of the display driver and hardware-specific details.
The commit message clearly describes the technical changes made to fix a CFI violation in the kernel code related to AMD GPUs.
The commit message indicates that it corrects a mistake in the dt-bindings for pinctrl on STM32 devices. It changes the 'reset' property name to 'resets', which is the correct binding name.
The commit message clearly describes what the code does and its technical changes.

The commit message indicates that this change is related to power management (pm_runtime) for the sun8i-ss platform. The code changes are focused on simplifying the code by replacing pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get, which is a more appropriate function for this purpose.

The commit message indicates that this change is related to io_uring, which is a new interface for asynchronous I/O operations in the Linux kernel. The technical changes include splitting out fixed file installation and removal code from the main io_uring implementation into a separate filetable code.
The commit message clearly describes the technical changes made to the media driver bindings for NXP's VPU decoder blocks.

The commit message indicates that it removes a macro used in configuring Intel Wi-Fi devices (IWL_DEVICE_BZ). The technical changes include removing the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro and combining its contents with the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro. This suggests a primary purpose of simplifying or refactoring code, which is consistent with the PRC category.
